title: Buttermilk Biscuits
description: Flaky, buttery, golden biscuits with just the right rise. Soft inside, crisp outside, and built for everything from breakfast to full-on comfort meals.
ingredients: 7 tbsp unsalted butter, chilled and sliced
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Whisk together flour, baking powder, salt, and baking soda.
Cut butter into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Make a well and add buttermilk. Stir until just combined.
Turn dough onto a floured surface and form into a rectangle.
Fold into thirds, rotate, and repeat folding two more times.
Roll or pat dough to about 1/2 inch thick.
Cut into biscuits and place on baking sheet.
Press a slight indent into each biscuit and brush with buttermilk.
Bake for about 15 minutes until golden brown.
notes: Keep everything cold for the best texture.
Do not overwork the dough or biscuits will be tough.
Place biscuits close together for softer sides, spaced apart for crisp edges.

variations.title: Buttermilk Biscuits V2
instructions: Combine dry ingredients in a bowl or food processor.
Cut or pulse butter into flour until coarse meal forms.
Add buttermilk and mix just until combined. Dough should be slightly wet.
Turn onto floured surface and gently pat to 1/2 inch thick.
Fold dough about 5 times and press to about 1 inch thick.
Cut into rounds and place on baking sheet.
Bake 10 to 12 minutes until golden brown.
notes: Handle dough as little as possible to keep biscuits tender.
Pat by hand, do not use a rolling pin.
Freeze unbaked biscuits and bake straight from frozen at 450°F for about 20 minutes.

whyThisHits: This is technique-driven cooking. When you get it right, you get layers, lift, and that perfect bite that makes everything else on the plate better.
pairing: Serve with butter and jam, sausage gravy, fried chicken, or just eat one straight off the tray while nobody is looking.
footerLine: Art for Your Mouth. Because boring food is a waste of time.
